extra.reasoning: The property (setting fever true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED. The fever rule interaction_drop has priority 100, which dominates every other rule (priorities 2 and 3). Whenever fever is true, interaction_drop fires and wins conflict resolution, forcing acuity := self_care (the lowest enum value), overriding any raise from leg_swelling or abdominal_pain. Counterexample with leg_swelling=true, abdominal_pain=false: with fever=FALSE, only raise_leg_swelling matches, so acuity = emergent (highest). Flipping fever to TRUE (other inputs unchanged) makes interaction_drop (priority 100) win over raise_leg_swelling (priority 3), giving acuity = self_care (lowest). Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, so fever=true lowered acuity. The witness assignment is the fever=true state demonstrating acuity=self_care; the same inputs with fever=false yield emergent, establishing the monotonicity violation. (Any case with leg_swelling or abdominal_pain set would similarly violate.)
